To what extent does video game gambling affect the likelihood of lack of

subject.title: Psychology
subject.description:
IB Psychology is a Group 3 subject in the International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP). The course examines human behavior and mental processes through scientific inquiry, exploring biological, cognitive, and sociocultural approaches to understanding behavior. Students develop critical thinking skills while investigating contemporary psychological research, research methods, and ethical considerations in psychological practice.

The course is available at Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours) and Higher Level (HL, 240 hours), with first assessment in 2027.

Assessment consists of Paper 1 (integration of concepts, content, and contexts through short-answer and extended-response questions: SL 2 hours, HL 2 hours, SL 50%, HL 40%), Paper 2 (application of psychological knowledge to research contexts: SL 1 hour, HL 2 hours, SL 25%, HL 20%), Paper 3 (HL only: research methods and data analysis, 1 hour, 20%), and Internal Assessment (research proposal development: SL 25%, HL 20%).

strengths: The title and research question are clearly stated and remain central throughout, providing a coherent focus.
The introduction situates the investigation within a relevant social and scientific context and outlines the essayâs organisation effectively.
Methodological choices (peerâreviewed studies, theoretical models) are generally well justified, showing evidence of informed source selection.
weaknesses: Minor typographical and pagination errors occasionally weaken clarity.
No explicit description of search strategy or inclusion/exclusion criteria.
Lacks discussion of ethical considerations or a fully systematic approach to methodology.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student demonstrates very good communication of topic and purpose, with a focused research question that is maintained throughout (Band 5â6). The methodology is mostly complete and relevant but lacks systematic rigour (Band 3â4). On balance, a mark of 5/6 accurately reflects strong performance with minor lapses in methodological detail and occasional expression errors.

strengths: Solid application of key psychological concepts (orbitofrontal cortex, reinforcement schedules, pathway model) to the research question.
Consistent use of specialised terminology (PGSI, BIS-Brief, variable-ratio) linked to theoretical frameworks.
Broad and relevant bibliography, demonstrating thorough subject knowledge and source integration.
weaknesses: Occasional conflation of impulsivity and problem gambling constructs, leading to slight conceptual imprecision.
Minor inconsistencies in APA in-text referencing and punctuation.
A few definitional details could be tightened to avoid ambiguity.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work shows very good subject knowledge and coherent application of sources (Band 5â6) and employs terminology accurately for the most part (Band 5â6). Occasional imprecisions and formatting inconsistencies prevent a topâband (6) but justify 5/6.

strengths: Well-balanced presentation of supporting and opposing evidence, demonstrating critical engagement.
Clear acknowledgement and evaluation of study limitations (sample bias, design constraints).
Forward-looking recommendations (experimental designs, validated scales) show evaluative depth and planning capacity.
weaknesses: Analysis remains somewhat descriptive in places, lacking deeper synthesis across studies.
No statistical effect sizes or confidence intervals are discussed to strengthen analytical rigour.
Some sections (cultural generalisability, interview biases) require tighter integration into the main argument.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The essay exhibits good research relevance and a reasoned, structured argument with partially critical evaluation (Band 7â9). However, analysis is not consistently deep and synthesis across sources is uneven, placing the work in the midâupper range of the band. A mark of 8/12 reflects these strengths and limitations.

strengths: The report follows conventional extended essay structure (title page, TOC, headings, bibliography).
Headings and subheadings guide the reader effectively through sections.
weaknesses: Inconsistent pagination in the table of contents versus actual section starts.
Citation formatting is uneven; bibliography entries lack uniform APA style.
The word count exceeds the 4,000-word limit and pages are not clearly numbered throughout.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Structure is generally appropriate but marred by pagination mismatches, inconsistent formatting, and failure to meet word-count conventions (Band 1â2). The work meets only some basic layout requirements, warranting 2/4.

strandSelections.reasoning: Specialised terms (e.g., PGSI, BIS-Brief, variable-ratio schedules, social desirability bias) are employed accurately and repeatedly.  Concepts are linked to appropriate theoretical frameworks, and APA-style in-text references are attempted.  Spelling errors and the occasional misuse of punctuation show minor inconsistency, so the strand sits at 5 rather than 6.

Basic layout elements are present (title, word count, headings), yet the word count exceeds the 4,000-word maximum, citation formatting is inconsistent, and pages are not clearly numbered within the document extract.  These shortcomings mean some but not all layout conventions are met, placing the work in the 1-2 band at 1.

The student demonstrates solid knowledge of psychological concepts such as the orbitofrontal cortex, variable-ratio reinforcement, problem gambling severity, and Blaszczynski & Nowerâs pathway model, applying them coherently to the research question.  Empirical studies are summarised accurately and generally cited correctly.  Occasional definitional imprecision (e.g., conflating impulsivity with problem gambling) prevents a 6, but the grasp of subject matter and source integration is consistently strong.

The essay employs a literature-review methodology, selecting multiple peer-reviewed studies as supporting and opposing evidence and including two theoretical models.  Source choice is generally relevant and the student justifies inclusion (sample size, triangulation, gender balance).  However, the methodology remains secondary and descriptive; there is no evidence of a systematic search strategy, inclusion/exclusion criteria, or explicit evaluation of ethical considerations.  Consequently, the methodology is âmostly completeâ rather than fully developed, fitting the 3-4 band.

The student states a precise title, defines key constructs (loot boxes, skin gambling, impulsivity) and situates the topic within adolescent mental-health concerns.  The introduction explains why the issue is socially and scientifically relevant and outlines how the essay will be organised, allowing the reader to follow the line of inquiry easily.  Minor lapses in expression and pagination do not obscure meaning, so communication remains clear and appropriate, meriting the upper band, though not the top of the band because clarity is occasionally weakened by typographical errors.

A wide range of contemporary journal articles, models and statistics are incorporated, and almost all are directly relevant to the stated question.  The student deliberately contrasts supporting and opposing evidence, enhancing relevance.  Some sources, however, focus on adults rather than adolescents, and grey literature (web articles) appears sporadically, lowering consistency and keeping the work to the lower end of the 7-9 band.

The research question â âTo what extent does video-game gambling affect the likelihood of lack of impulse control in adolescents?â â is explicitly presented at the start and is referred back to throughout the discussion and conclusion.  Its scope is sufficiently narrow (specific behaviour, population, psychological construct) to remain manageable, and every section attempts to connect evidence to that focus.  Some drifting toward adult samples is noted, yet linkage back to adolescents is regularly made, so overall focus is maintained effectively.

The essay follows conventional EE structure (title page, table of contents, introduction, discussion, conclusion, bibliography).  Headings and sub-headings guide the reader and internal coherence is adequate.  Page numbers in the contents do not always match section breaks, and some figure placement is awkward, but overall the structure is appropriate, earning the lower half of the top band.

The essay is organised into supporting and opposing sections with a final comparative conclusion that weighs evidence and recognises methodological weaknesses.  The argument progresses logically from models to empirical data and ends with recommendations for future research, demonstrating a critical stance.  Evaluation is present but not consistently deep (e.g., no statistical effect sizes, no meta-analytic comparison), so the work fits the lower end of the 7-9 band.

For each study the student identifies strengths (sample size, triangulation) and limitations (self-report bias, cultural specificity) and attempts to link findings back to impulsivity.  Nevertheless, much of the commentary is descriptive, and causal reasoning is acknowledged but not resolved analytically.  Synthesis across studies is uneven, so the analysis is âclearâ and âmostly supportedâ but not yet âeffectiveâ or thoroughly integrated, placing it mid-band at 5.

annotations.comment: The title succinctly communicates the topic and focus, guiding the reader effectively into the investigation.
The word count exceeds the 4,000-word limit and page numbers are not clearly displayed on this page. Ensure compliance with IB formatting rules.
The research question is clearly stated and remains central; this precision supports a coherent focus throughout the essay.
The table of contents shows inconsistencies between listed page numbers and actual section starts. Verify and align TOC entries with document pagination.
The structure of the contents is clear and follows conventions, aiding navigation through the sections.
Definitions of loot boxes, skins and skin gambling are thorough, demonstrating strong subject knowledge and clarity in concepts.
The pathway descriptions become repetitive. Consider synthesising key points across pathways to streamline the argument.
Greer et al. (2022) model inclusion demonstrates effective source integration but could benefit from more analytical synthesis.
The study limitations are critically acknowledged, reflecting thoughtful evaluation of generalizability to adolescents.
The introduction occasionally conflates impulsivity with problem gambling. Distinguish these constructs explicitly to avoid conceptual confusion.
The paragraph on adolescent prevalence provides relevant statistics but lacks critical appraisal of source reliability.
The transition into methodology lacks a description of search strategy and inclusion criteria. Outline these to strengthen methodological rigour.
The overview of supporting and opposing studies demonstrates critical engagement with different perspectives early on.
The sentence is incomplete (âWardle and Zendle (2020) are specifically related since theyâ¦â). Complete or restructure it to improve clarity.
Figure 1 is well chosen to illustrate the theoretical model but its placement interrupts the narrative flow.
The caption correctly cites Blaszczynski & Nower (2002) but lacks APA formatting consistency; adjust to standard style.
Discussion of Pathway Model shows deep understanding of theoretical frameworks and links to impulsivity mechanisms.
Spicer et al. uses an adult sample, limiting direct applicability to adolescent impulsivity. Narrow sample selection to improve relevance.
The critique of cross-sectional design effectively highlights the need for longitudinal or experimental methods.
King et al. sample composition skews male and adult, reducing its relevance to adolescent impulsivity. Address demographic imbalance.
The case study discussion acknowledges both strengths and limitations, demonstrating balanced critical evaluation.
The unlabeled footnote number â12â near the margin is confusing. Ensure footnotes are placed and numbered consistently.
The conclusion effectively synthesizes supporting and opposing evidence but repeats earlier discussion points.
No effect sizes or confidence intervals are reported for the survey results. Including these statistics would strengthen analytical rigor.
Triangulation of survey and interview data is well justified and enhances the validity of Wardle & Zendleâs findings.
The lengthy critique of interview biases reads descriptively. Synthesize limitations more concisely and critically.
Rockloff et al.âs balanced gender sample provides strong generalizability to female populations.
The study did not directly measure impulsivity, only inferred it from gambling harm. Suggest adding a validated impulsivity scale.
The summary of survey-only methodology limitations shows awareness of causality issues and participant bias.
The conclusion at this stage reiterates evidence descriptively without integrating it into an overarching argument. Refocus on addressing the research question directly.
The recommendation for experimental designs demonstrates forward-thinking and capacity to plan further inquiry.
The extended discussion of cultural generalizability on page 15 is insightful but could be better integrated into the discussion section.
Bibliographic entries show inconsistent formatting and missing hanging indents; revise to conform to APA style guidelines.
The bibliography demonstrates thorough research and a wide range of sources, enhancing the investigationâs credibility.
Web references are not consistently italicized or formatted; ensure titles of websites and reports follow APA conventions.
The extensive list of sources shows excellent breadth but could be pruned to the most directly relevant studies.
The final bibliographic continuation maintains consistent formatting with preceding entries, showing attention to detail.
